name: ipsec-vpn
title: OCI Hybrid Connectivity — Site-to-Site IPSec VPN
summary: Creates CPE objects and IPSec connections terminating on a DRG, with per-tunnel configuration (BGP or static, PSK, IKE version) via tunnel management.

governance.name: prefix
governance.description: Short organization or project prefix. First token of every resource name: {prefix}-{cloud}-{environment}-{resource-type}-{suffix}.

governance.name: environment
governance.description: Deployment environment identifier (e.g., dev, test, prod). Used in resource names and mandatory freeform tags.

governance.name: owner
governance.description: Team or individual that owns the deployed resources. Applied as the mandatory 'owner' freeform tag.

governance.name: cost_center
governance.description: Cost center code for chargeback/showback. Applied as the mandatory 'cost_center' freeform tag.

governance.name: additional_tags
governance.description: Optional additional freeform tags merged beneath the mandatory tag set. Mandatory tags win on key collision.

governance.name: name_suffix
governance.description: Final token of every resource name, used to disambiguate multiple instances of this module.

module.name: compartment_id
module.description: OCID of the compartment for the CPE and IPSec connections.

module.name: drg_id
module.description: OCID of the DRG terminating the VPN. May come from any source (oci/transit/drg output, existing DRG) — no framework dependency is implied.

module.name: customer_premises_equipment
module.description: CPE objects (on-premises devices) keyed by logical name.

module.name: ipsec_connections
module.type: map(object({ cpe_key = string static_routes = optional(list(string), []) tunnels = optional(map(object({ tunnel_index = number routing_type = optional(string, "BGP") shared_secret = optional(string) ike_version = optional(string, "V2") customer_bgp_asn = optional(string) oracle_interface_ip = optional(string) customer_interface_ip = optional(string) })), {}) }))
module.description: IPSec connections keyed by logical name, each referencing a CPE. OCI always provisions TWO tunnels per connection; entries in tunnels (keyed 1:1 by tunnel_index) configure BGP/PSK per tunnel via tunnel management. static_routes must be non-empty when any tunnel uses STATIC routing.

outputs.name: gateway_id
outputs.description: OCID of the DRG terminating the VPN (as supplied).

outputs.name: cpe_ids
outputs.description: Map of logical CPE name to CPE OCID.

outputs.name: connection_ids
outputs.description: Map of logical IPSec connection name to IPSec connection OCID.

outputs.name: tunnel_oracle_ips
outputs.description: Map of logical connection name to the Oracle-side VPN headend IPs of both tunnels. Hand these to the on-premises device configuration.

outputs.name: tunnel_status
outputs.description: Map of logical connection name to both tunnels' lifecycle/status.
